titleStmt.title: SII 25.235: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 235.

summary.p: Slab set up near the police station, being brought from Jaṅgla-Pōtēnār.
This incomplete record may palaeographically be assigned to about the 12th Century A.D.

It states that the king belonged to the Nāgavaṁśa and to the Kāśyapagōtra. He is mentioned as the lord of Bhōgavatī, whose crest is a tiger with a calf and who is the worshipper of the feet of Māṇikyadēvī.
It is possible that the Nāgavaṁśi rulers worshipped Durgā under the name of Māṇikyadēvī. (See Ep. Ind., Vol. X, p. 27).
It further mentions a son (name not given) of [mahā]maṇḍalēśvara Raṁgāyadēva-mahārāju, whose identity is not known.
Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. X, No. 649.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī saha
sra phaṇāmaṇiki
raṇanika*rāvabhāsu
ra nāgavaṁśōdbhava bhō
gā vatīpura va
rādhīśvara savatsa
vyāghralāṁcha
na kāśyapagō
tra prakaṭīkṛta
vijaya ghōṣaṇa

viśva viśvaṁbha
rādhīśvara pa
ramēśvara
parama bha
ṭṭāraka mahā
mahēśvara
caraṇa kaṁja
kiṁjalka puṁja
piṁjarita bhra
ma rāyamā
na mānōnnata śrī
māṇikyadēvīdi

vya śrīpādapadmārādha
ka parabala śādhaka
śrīmajjagadēkabhū
ṣaṇa mahārā
ju lēta śrīmavardhaShould be read as ‘śrīmanmahā’
maṁḍalēśvara raṁ
gāyadēva ma
hārājula ko
ḍukuStops abruptly.
Digital edition of SII 25.235 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
